    19ŌåÆ/-!
    20ŌåÆ# Erd┼æs Problem 852
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/852](https://www.erdosproblems.com/852)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆLet $d_n = p_{n+1} - p_n$, where $p_n$ is the $n$-th prime. Let $h(x)$ be maximal
    25ŌåÆsuch that for some $n < x$ the numbers $d_n, d_{n+1}, \ldots, d_{n+h(x)-1}$ are
    26ŌåÆall distinct. Estimate $h(x)$.
    27ŌåÆ
    28ŌåÆBrun's sieve implies $h(x) \to \infty$ as $x \to \infty$.
    29ŌåÆ
    30ŌåÆ[Er85c] Erd┼æs, P., _Some problems and results on combinatorial number theory_ (1985).
    31ŌåÆ-/
    32ŌåÆ
    33ŌåÆnamespace Erdos852
    34ŌåÆ
    35ŌåÆ/-- The $n$-th prime (0-indexed): $p_0 = 2$, $p_1 = 3$, etc. -/
    36ŌåÆnoncomputable def nthPrime (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ := Nat.nth Nat.Prime n
    37ŌåÆ
    38ŌåÆ/-- The $n$-th prime gap: $d_n = p_{n+1} - p_n$. -/
    39ŌåÆnoncomputable def primeGap (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ := nthPrime (n + 1) - nthPrime n
    40ŌåÆ
    41ŌåÆ/-- A run of $k$ consecutive prime gaps starting at position $n$ are all distinct:
    42ŌåÆ$d_n, d_{n+1}, \ldots, d_{n+k-1}$ are pairwise different. -/
    43ŌåÆdef IsDistinctPrimeGapRun (n k : ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
    44ŌåÆ  Function.Injective (fun i : Fin k => primeGap (n + i.val))
    45ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆ/-- $h(x)$: the maximal length of a run of distinct consecutive prime gaps
    47ŌåÆ$d_n, d_{n+1}, \ldots, d_{n+h-1}$ for some $n < x$. -/
    48ŌåÆnoncomputable def h (x : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
    49ŌåÆ  sSup {k : ŌäĢ | Ōłā n, n < x Ōł¦ IsDistinctPrimeGapRun n k}
    50ŌåÆ
    51ŌåÆ/--
    52ŌåÆ**Erd┼æs Problem 852** ŌĆö Lower bound [Er85c]:
    53ŌåÆ
    54ŌåÆIs it true that there exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $x$,
    55ŌåÆ$h(x) > (\log x)^c$?
    56ŌåÆ-/
    57ŌåÆ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    58ŌåÆtheorem erdos_852 : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    59ŌåÆ    Ōłā c : ŌäØ, c > 0 Ōł¦
    60ŌåÆ    Ōłā NŌéĆ : ŌäĢ, ŌłĆ x : ŌäĢ, x Ōēź NŌéĆ ŌåÆ
    61ŌåÆ      (h x : ŌäØ) > (Real.log (x : ŌäØ)) ^ c := by
    62ŌåÆ  sorry
    63ŌåÆ
    64ŌåÆ/--
    65ŌåÆ**Erd┼æs Problem 852** ŌĆö Upper bound [Er85c]:
    66ŌåÆ
    67ŌåÆIs it true that $h(x) = o(\log x)$, i.e., for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $N_0$
    68ŌåÆsuch that for all $x \geq N_0$, $h(x) < \varepsilon \cdot \log(x)$?
    69ŌåÆ-/
    70ŌåÆ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    71ŌåÆtheorem erdos_852.variants.upper_bound : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    72ŌåÆ    ŌłĆ ╬Ą : ŌäØ, ╬Ą > 0 ŌåÆ
    73ŌåÆ    Ōłā NŌéĆ : ŌäĢ, ŌłĆ x : ŌäĢ, x Ōēź NŌéĆ ŌåÆ
    74ŌåÆ      (h x : ŌäØ) < ╬Ą * Real.log (x : ŌäØ) := by

    19ŌåÆ/-!
    20ŌåÆ# Erd┼æs Problem 854
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/854](https://www.erdosproblems.com/854)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆLet $n_k$ denote the $k$-th primorial, i.e. the product of the first $k$ primes.
    25ŌåÆIf $1 = a_1 < a_2 < \cdots < a_{\varphi(n_k)} = n_k - 1$ is the sequence of integers
    26ŌåÆcoprime to $n_k$, are there $\gg \max_i (a_{i+1} - a_i)$ many even integers
    27ŌåÆof the form $a_{j+1} - a_j$?
    28ŌåÆ
    29ŌåÆThis was asked by Erd┼æs in Oberwolfach (most likely in 1986) [Er85c, Ob1].
    30ŌåÆ-/
    31ŌåÆ
    32ŌåÆopen Nat Finset
    33ŌåÆ
    34ŌåÆnamespace Erdos854
    35ŌåÆ
    36ŌåÆ/-- The $k$-th primorial: product of the first $k$ primes.
    37ŌåÆ$\operatorname{primorial}(0) = 1$, $\operatorname{primorial}(1) = 2$,
    38ŌåÆ$\operatorname{primorial}(2) = 6$, $\operatorname{primorial}(3) = 30$. -/
    39ŌåÆnoncomputable def primorial : ŌäĢ ŌåÆ ŌäĢ
    40ŌåÆ  | 0 => 1
    41ŌåÆ  | k + 1 => Nat.nth Nat.Prime k * primorial k
    42ŌåÆ
    43ŌåÆ/-- The sorted list of integers in $\{1, \ldots, n-1\}$ coprime to $n$. -/
    44ŌåÆnoncomputable def coprimeList (n : ŌäĢ) : List ŌäĢ :=
    45ŌåÆ  ((Finset.range n).filter (fun a => 0 < a Ōł¦ Nat.Coprime a n)).sort (┬Ę Ōēż ┬Ę)
    46ŌåÆ
    47ŌåÆ/-- Consecutive differences of a sorted list of natural numbers. -/
    48ŌåÆdef consecutiveDiffs : List ŌäĢ ŌåÆ List ŌäĢ
    49ŌåÆ  | [] => []
    50ŌåÆ  | [_] => []
    51ŌåÆ  | a :: b :: rest => (b - a) :: consecutiveDiffs (b :: rest)
    52ŌåÆ
    53ŌåÆ/-- The set of distinct gap values in the coprime sequence for $n$. -/
    54ŌåÆnoncomputable def gapValues (n : ŌäĢ) : Finset ŌäĢ :=
    55ŌåÆ  (consecutiveDiffs (coprimeList n)).toFinset
    56ŌåÆ
    57ŌåÆ/-- The maximum consecutive gap in the coprime sequence for $n$. -/
    58ŌåÆnoncomputable def maxGap (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
    59ŌåÆ  (consecutiveDiffs (coprimeList n)).foldl max 0
    60ŌåÆ
    61ŌåÆ/--
    62ŌåÆErd┼æs Problem 854 [Er85c, Ob1]:
    63ŌåÆ
    64ŌåÆLet $n_k$ be the $k$-th primorial. The number of distinct even integers appearing
    65ŌåÆas consecutive gaps among integers coprime to $n_k$ in $\{1, \ldots, n_k - 1\}$
    66ŌåÆis $\gg \max_i(a_{i+1} - a_i)$. That is, there exist $C > 0$ and $K_0$ such that for all
    67ŌåÆ$k \geq K_0$, the number of distinct gap values is at least $C$ times the maximum gap.
    68ŌåÆ-/
    69ŌåÆ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    70ŌåÆtheorem erdos_854 : answer(sorry) Ōåö
    71ŌåÆ    Ōłā C : ŌäØ, C > 0 Ōł¦ Ōłā KŌéĆ : ŌäĢ, ŌłĆ k : ŌäĢ, k Ōēź KŌéĆ ŌåÆ
    72ŌåÆ      (((gapValues (primorial k)).filter (Even ┬Ę)).card : ŌäØ) Ōēź
    73ŌåÆ        C * (maxGap (primorial k) : ŌäØ) := by
    74ŌåÆ  sorry
    75ŌåÆ
    76ŌåÆend Erdos854

    18ŌåÆ
    19ŌåÆ/-!
    20ŌåÆ# Erd┼æs Problem 5
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/5](https://www.erdosproblems.com/5)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆThis problem concerns the set of limit points of the normalized prime gap sequence
    25ŌåÆ$(p_{n+1} - p_n) / \log n$, where $p_n$ denotes the $n$-th prime. The conjecture,
    26ŌåÆposed in several papers of Erd┼æs [Er55c, Er57, Er61, Er65b, Er85c, Er90, Er97c],
    27ŌåÆasserts that this set of limit points equals $[0, \infty]$.
    28ŌåÆ-/
    29ŌåÆ
    30ŌåÆopen Filter Nat Real
    31ŌåÆ
    32ŌåÆnamespace Erdos5
    33ŌåÆ
    34ŌåÆ/--
    35ŌåÆThe normalized prime gap at index $n$ (0-indexed):
    36ŌåÆ$$\frac{p_{n+1} - p_n}{\log(n+1)}$$
    37ŌåÆwhere $p_n = \texttt{nth Nat.Prime}\; n$ is the $n$-th prime (so $p_0 = 2$, $p_1 = 3$, ...).
    38ŌåÆWe use $n+1$ in the denominator so that $\log$ is evaluated at a positive argument.
    39ŌåÆ-/
    40ŌåÆnoncomputable def normalizedPrimeGap (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäØ :=
    41ŌåÆ  ((nth Nat.Prime (n + 1) : ŌäØ) - (nth Nat.Prime n : ŌäØ)) / Real.log ((n : ŌäØ) + 1)
    42ŌåÆ
    43ŌåÆ/--
    44ŌåÆLet $p_n$ denote the $n$-th prime. Let $S$ be the set of limit points of the sequence
    45ŌåÆ$(p_{n+1} - p_n) / \log n$.
    46ŌåÆThe conjecture [Er55c, Er57, Er61, Er65b, Er85c, Er90, Er97c] asserts $S = [0, \infty]$,
    47ŌåÆi.e., every value $C \in [0, \infty]$ is attained as a limit along some subsequence.
    48ŌåÆ
    49ŌåÆFormally (for finite $C$): for every $C \geq 0$ there exists a strictly increasing
    50ŌåÆsequence of indices $n_1 < n_2 < \cdots$ such that
    51ŌåÆ$$(p_{n_i+1} - p_{n_i}) / \log n_i \to C \quad \text{as } i \to \infty.$$
    52ŌåÆ
    53ŌåÆKnown results toward this conjecture:
    54ŌåÆ- $\infty \in S$ (Westzynthius 1931): prime gaps are unbounded relative to $\log n$.
    55ŌåÆ- $0 \in S$ (GoldstonŌĆōPintzŌĆōY─▒ld─▒r─▒m 2009): normalized gaps can be arbitrarily small.
    56ŌåÆ- $S$ has positive Lebesgue measure (Erd┼æs 1955; Ricci 1956).
    57ŌåÆ- $S$ contains arbitrarily large finite numbers (HildebrandŌĆōMaier 1988).
    58ŌåÆ- $[0, c] \subseteq S$ for some $c > 0$ (Pintz 2016).
    59ŌåÆ- At least $12.5\%$ of $[0, \infty)$ belongs to $S$ (BanksŌĆōFreibergŌĆōMaynard 2016).
    60ŌåÆ- At least $1/3$ of $[0, \infty)$ belongs to $S$, and $S$ has bounded gaps (Merikoski 2020).
    61ŌåÆ-/
    62ŌåÆ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    63ŌåÆtheorem erdos_5 :
    64ŌåÆ    ŌłĆ C : ŌäØ, 0 Ōēż C ŌåÆ
    65ŌåÆ      Ōłā f : ŌäĢ ŌåÆ ŌäĢ, StrictMono f Ōł¦
    66ŌåÆ        Tendsto (fun i => normalizedPrimeGap (f i)) atTop (nhds C) := by
    67ŌåÆ  sorry
    68ŌåÆ
    69ŌåÆend Erdos5
    70ŌåÆ

    32ŌåÆdenotes the prime gap function.

    40ŌåÆFor all $m$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that $d_n < d_{n+1} < \dots < d_{n+m}$,
    41ŌåÆwhere $d$ denotes the prime gap function.

    48ŌåÆ    {n | ŌłĆ i Ōłł Finset.range m, primeGap (n + i) < primeGap (n + i + 1)}.Infinite := by
    49ŌåÆ  sorry

    54ŌåÆwhere $d$ denotes the prime gap function.

    61ŌåÆ    {n | ŌłĆ i Ōłł Finset.range m, primeGap (n + i) > primeGap (n + i + 1)}.Infinite := by

    20ŌåÆ# Erd┼æs Problem 235
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/235](https://www.erdosproblems.com/235)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆLet $N_k = p_1 \cdots p_k$ be the $k$-th primorial and let $a_1 < \cdots < a_{\varphi(N_k)}$
    25ŌåÆbe the integers less than $N_k$ coprime to $N_k$. Erd┼æs conjectured that the fraction of
    26ŌåÆconsecutive gaps $a_{i+1} - a_i \le c \cdot N_k / \varphi(N_k)$ tends to a limit that is a
    27ŌåÆcontinuous function of $c$. This was proved by Hooley.
    28ŌåÆ
    29ŌåÆ[Er55c] Erd┼æs, P., _Some problems on number theory_ (1955).
    30ŌåÆ
    31ŌåÆ[Ho65] Hooley, C., _On the difference of consecutive numbers prime to n_, Acta Arith. 8 (1963),
    32ŌåÆ343-347.
    33ŌåÆ-/
    34ŌåÆ
    35ŌåÆopen Filter Nat Classical
    36ŌåÆ
    37ŌåÆopen scoped BigOperators
    38ŌåÆ
    39ŌåÆnamespace Erdos235
    40ŌåÆ
    41ŌåÆ/-- The $k$-th primorial: product of the first $k$ primes ($p_1 \cdot p_2 \cdots p_k$). -/
    42ŌåÆnoncomputable def primorial (k : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ := ŌłÅ i Ōłł Finset.range k, nth Nat.Prime i
    43ŌåÆ
    44ŌåÆ/-- The sorted list of natural numbers less than $n$ that are coprime to $n$. -/
    45ŌåÆdef sortedCoprimes (n : ŌäĢ) : List ŌäĢ :=
    46ŌåÆ  ((Finset.range n).filter (fun a => Nat.Coprime a n)).sort (┬Ę Ōēż ┬Ę)
    47ŌåÆ
    48ŌåÆ/-- Count consecutive gaps $\le$ threshold in a sorted list of naturals. -/
    49ŌåÆnoncomputable def countSmallGaps : List ŌäĢ ŌåÆ ŌäØ ŌåÆ ŌäĢ
    50ŌåÆ  | [], _ => 0
    51ŌåÆ  | [_], _ => 0
    52ŌåÆ  | a :: b :: rest, threshold =>
    53ŌåÆ    (if ((b : ŌäØ) - (a : ŌäØ)) Ōēż threshold then 1 else 0) + countSmallGaps (b :: rest) threshold
    54ŌåÆ
    55ŌåÆ/-- The fraction of consecutive gaps among reduced residues $\bmod N_k$
    56ŌåÆthat are at most $c \cdot N_k / \varphi(N_k)$. -/
    57ŌåÆnoncomputable def gapFraction (k : ŌäĢ) (c : ŌäØ) : ŌäØ :=
    58ŌåÆ  let Nk := primorial k
    59ŌåÆ  let residues := sortedCoprimes Nk
    60ŌåÆ  let threshold := c * (Nk : ŌäØ) / (Nat.totient Nk : ŌäØ)
    61ŌåÆ  (countSmallGaps residues threshold : ŌäØ) / (Nat.totient Nk : ŌäØ)
    62ŌåÆ
    63ŌåÆ/--
    64ŌåÆErd┼æs Problem 235 [Er55c] (proved by Hooley [Ho65]):
    65ŌåÆ
    66ŌåÆLet $N_k = 2 \cdot 3 \cdots p_k$ (the $k$-th primorial) and let
    67ŌåÆ$a_1 < a_2 < \cdots < a_{\varphi(N_k)}$ be the integers less than $N_k$ coprime to $N_k$.
    68ŌåÆThen for any $c \ge 0$, the limit
    69ŌåÆ$$
    70ŌåÆ\lim_{k \to \infty} \frac{\#\{i : a_i - a_{i-1} \le c \cdot N_k / \varphi(N_k)\}}{\varphi(N_k)}
    71ŌåÆ$$
    72ŌåÆexists and defines a continuous function of $c$.
    73ŌåÆ
    74ŌåÆHooley proved that the limiting distribution is exponential: $f(c) = 1 - e^{-c}$.
    75ŌåÆ-/
    76ŌåÆ@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    77ŌåÆtheorem erdos_235 :
    78ŌåÆ    Ōłā f : ŌäØ ŌåÆ ŌäØ, Continuous f Ōł¦
    79ŌåÆ      ŌłĆ c : ŌäØ, 0 Ōēż c ŌåÆ
    80ŌåÆ        Tendsto (fun k => gapFraction k c) atTop (nhds (f c)) := by
    81ŌåÆ  sorry
    82ŌåÆ
    83ŌåÆend Erdos235
